entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor, used as straw for composting and evaluated for compost maturity

entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: Eleusine coracana, used as straw for composting

entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: Glycine max, used as straw for composting and as seeds for germination assays

entities.name: TOMATO
entities.description: Used as seeds for germination assays to test compost maturity

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Used as seeds for germination assays to test compost maturity

entities.name: COMPOSTING
entities.description: Aerobic bioconversion of agricultural residues into nutrient-rich products

entities.name: PHYSICO-CHEMICAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analysis used to evaluate the decomposition pattern and quality of composts

entities.name: MICROBIAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analysis used to evaluate the decomposition pattern and quality of composts

entities.name: STATISTICAL ANALYSIS
entities.description: Analysis used to evaluate the decomposition pattern and quality of composts

entities.name: SEED GERMINATION ASSAY
entities.description: Assay used to determine the germination index and assess phytotoxicity and maturity

entities.name: GERMINATION INDEX
entities.description: A threshold value used as a maturity index for composts

entities.name: PHYTOTOXICITY
entities.description: The toxic effect of compost on seed germination

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The state of compost being stable and free from phytotoxicity

entities.name: C/N RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of carbon to nitrogen used to characterize the decomposition of composting materials

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to provide nutrients for plant growth, enhanced by compost

entities.name: MICROBIAL DIVERSITY
entities.description: The variety of microorganisms in the soil, enhanced by compost

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The overall output of agricultural crops, improved by soil fertility and microbial diversity

entities.name: SAW DUST
entities.description: Material added to agricultural residues to study its impact on decomposition and compost quality

entities.name: DECOMPOSITION PATTERN
entities.description: The process and characteristics of organic matter breakdown during composting
